keyPoints: Lightweight attentional-cost label as a non-intrusive cue to modulate proactive control.
Grounding in the Expected Value of Control framework and dopaminergic incentive effects, with effects contingent on task demand and individual differences.
Within-subject, randomized crossover 4-week design: label-on vs label-off blocks with washout.
Multi-modal outcomes: objective attention and switch metrics, productivity, NASA-TLX, plus optional physiological measures.
Minimal-salience UI encoding: progressive disclosure, consistency, and personalization options.
Preregistration and transparent reporting to enable null-result interpretation and replication.
